Most decisive NOT MET: expectation 1 (correlation identifier traceable across both modules). The response's entire cross-module tracing mechanism is built on `logging.getLogger().addFilter(ContextFilter())` — attaching the filter to the ROOT Logger object. But both ingest.py's and enrich.py's loggers are non-root child loggers (`logging.getLogger(__name__)`). In CPython's logging internals, `Logger.handle()` calls `self.filter(record)` only on the logger the call was made on; `callHandlers()` then walks up invoking each ancestor's *handlers* (and any handler-level filters), never re-invoking an ancestor Logger's own `.filter()`. So the root's ContextFilter never fires for records emitted by ingest.py's or enrich.py's loggers, meaning `record.run_id`/`record.record_id` are never actually set. The configured format string `%(run_id)s ... %(record_id)s` will then raise a KeyError when Formatter tries to substitute a missing attribute, for essentially every log call in both modules — so instead of showing the correlation IDs (as the response explicitly promises: "grep record_id=... app.log show[s] that record's full path across both files"), each log call would produce a logging-internal error dump, not the intended message. The showcased core feature is broken.
